---
title: "i_space"
author: Jascha Grübel
date: 2024-10-22
---

# iSPACE

# Setup

## Dev-Packages

```{r eval=F}
install.packages('remotes')
remotes::install_github("curso-r/treesnip@catboost",force=T)
remotes::install_url('https://github.com/catboost/catboost/releases/download/v1.2.10/catboost-R-windows-x86_64-1.2.10.tgz', INSTALL_opts = c("--no-multiarch", "--no-test-load", "--no-staged-install"))
```

## Packages

```{r}
library(caret)
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  #workflow
  tidyverse,caret,janitor,dplyr,rsample,recipes,parsnip,
  tune,dials,workflows,yardstick,bonsai,doParallel,fastDummies,
  #modelling
  randomForest,catboost,xgboost,lightgbm,
  #Visualisation
  ggplot2,vip,kernelshap,corrr,rcompanion,GGally,
  #SHAP
  shapviz,SHAPforxgboost) 

```

## Settings

```{r}
# speed up computation with parallel processing
all_cores <- parallel::detectCores(logical = FALSE) 
registerDoParallel(cores = all_cores) 

# set the random seed so we can reproduce any simulated results.
set.seed(1234)
```

## Functions

```{r}
`%notin%` <- Negate(`%in%`)

clean.labels<-function(x) {
  x %>%
    gsub("'", "", .) %>%
    gsub("\"", "", .) %>%
    gsub("%", "percent", .) %>%
    gsub("^[ ]+", "", .) %>%
    make.names(.) %>%
    gsub("[.]+", "_", .) %>%
    gsub("[_]+", "_", .) %>%
    tolower(.) %>%
    gsub("_$", "", .)
}

# Calculate a pairwise association between all variables in a data-frame. In particular nominal vs nominal with Chi-square, numeric vs numeric with Pearson correlation, and nominal vs numeric with ANOVA.
# Adopted from https://stackoverflow.com/a/52557631/590437
mixed_assoc = function(df, cor_method="spearman", adjust_cramersv_bias=TRUE){
    df_comb = expand.grid(names(df), names(df),  stringsAsFactors = F) %>% set_names("X1", "X2")
   
    is_nominal = function(x) class(x) %in% c("factor", "character")
    # https://community.rstudio.com/t/why-is-purr-is-numeric-deprecated/3559
    # https://github.com/r-lib/rlang/issues/781
    is_numeric <- function(x) { is.integer(x) || is_double(x)}

    f = function(xName,yName) {
        x =  pull(df, xName)
        y =  pull(df, yName)

        result = if(is_nominal(x) && is_nominal(y)){
            # use bias corrected cramersV as described in https://rdrr.io/cran/rcompanion/man/cramerV.html
            cv = cramerV(as.character(x), as.character(y), bias.correct = adjust_cramersv_bias)
            data.frame(xName, yName, assoc=cv, type="cramersV")

        }else if(is_numeric(x) && is_numeric(y)){
            correlation = cor(x, y, method=cor_method, use="complete.obs")
            data.frame(xName, yName, assoc=correlation, type="correlation")

        }else if(is_numeric(x) && is_nominal(y)){
            # from https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/119835/correlation-between-a-nominal-iv-and-a-continuous-dv-variable/124618#124618
            r_squared = summary(lm(x ~ y))$r.squared
            data.frame(xName, yName, assoc=sqrt(r_squared), type="anova")

        }else if(is_nominal(x) && is_numeric(y)){
            r_squared = summary(lm(y ~x))$r.squared
            data.frame(xName, yName, assoc=sqrt(r_squared), type="anova")

        }else {
            warning(paste("unmatched column type combination: ", class(x), class(y)))
        }

        # finally add complete obs number and ratio to table
        result %>% mutate(complete_obs_pairs=sum(!is.na(x) & !is.na(y)), complete_obs_ratio=complete_obs_pairs/length(x)) %>% rename(x=xName, y=yName)
    }

    # apply function to each variable combination
    map2_df(df_comb$X1, df_comb$X2, f)
}
```

## Data

### Load data

```{r}
filtered.raw <- readRDS("filtered.RDS") 

#TODO confert Visual_details (col 58) into meaningful categories  
cols_to_use <- c(1,4,5,8,10,12,
                 15,16,18:50,52:57,59,60)


filtered.data <- filtered.raw %>% select(all_of(cols_to_use)) %>%
  mutate(education = case_when(Educational_level == "University degree"~1,T~0),
         is_transition = case_when(isTransition == "Transition"~1,T~0),
         task = case_when(str_detect(Task,">")~"Waiting",T~Task),
         male = case_when(Gender == "Male"~1,T~0),
         right_handed = case_when(Handedness == "Right handed"~1,T~0),
         task = clean.labels(task) )%>%
  #dummy_cols(select_columns = "task",omit_colname_prefix = T)%>%
  select(#-task,
    -Task,-Educational_level,-Gender, -Gender_identity,-Age_Group,-isTransition,-Handedness)%>%
    janitor::clean_names() %>% mutate_if(is.character, factor) %>%
  filter(task=="training")
    
clean.raw <-readRDS("clean.RDS") 
clean.data <- clean.raw %>% select(all_of(cols_to_use)) %>%
  mutate(education = case_when(Educational_level == "University degree"~1,T~0),
         is_transition = case_when(isTransition == "Transition"~1,T~0),
         task = case_when(str_detect(Task,">")~"Waiting",T~Task),
         male = case_when(Gender == "Male"~1,T~0),
         right_handed = case_when(Handedness == "Right handed"~1,T~0),
         task = clean.labels(task) )%>%
  #dummy_cols(select_columns = "task",omit_colname_prefix = T)%>%
  select(#-task,
    -Task,-Educational_level,-Gender, -Gender_identity,-Age_Group,-isTransition,-Handedness)%>%
    janitor::clean_names() %>% mutate_if(is.character, factor)%>%
  filter(task=="training") %>% na.omit()


```

### Data inspection

```{r}

mydata.cor = cor(clean.data %>% select_if(is.numeric))
corrplot::corrplot(mydata.cor,order = "hclust",diag=T,tl.cex = 0.75)
reduced.data <- clean.data %>% 
  select(
    #Correlated with average duration
    -maximum_fixation_duration,-maximum_saccadic_duration,-max_blink_duration,
    #Snellen right correlated with left but left has less correlation with age
    -snellen_right,
    #Walking and physical are similar
    -walking,
    #L/R mean/Max highly correlated, use l_mean with lowest correlation to other variables
    -r_max,-r_mean,-l_max,
    #entropy, GTE; STE somewhat correlated but GTE/STE has same correlation structure
    -ste,
    #mean saccadic/fixation count highly correlated, use fixations
    -mean_saccadic_count,
    #counts correlated strongly with duration, only keep time
    -blink_count,-saccadic_count,-fixation_count,
    #L/R entropy/variation is highly correlated, use r_variation because is most different
    -l_entropy,-r_entropy,-l_variation,
    #aid strength l/r highly correlated, keep left as snellen kept right
    -aid_strength_left,
    #depression, anxiety, strees are highly correlated, keep depression for correlation with others
    -anxiety,-stress
    )


mydata.cor = cor(reduced.data %>% select_if(is.numeric))
corrplot::corrplot(mydata.cor,order = "hclust",diag=T,tl.cex = 0.75)

#ggpairs(clean.data %>% select(-participant,-task), aes(colour = task, alpha = 0.4))

# clean.data %>%
#     #select(- name) %>%
#     mixed_assoc() %>%
#     select(x, y, assoc) %>%
#     spread(y, assoc) %>%
#     column_to_rownames("x") %>%
#     as.matrix %>%
#     as_cordf %>%
#     network_plot()

```

### Split data

```{r}
reduced.split <- rsample::group_initial_split(
    reduced.data, 
    group = participant,
    prop = 0.7, 
    strata = mo_ca
)

filtered.split <- rsample::group_initial_split(
    filtered.data , 
    group = participant,
    prop = 0.8, 
    strata = mo_ca
)

clean.split <- rsample::group_initial_split(
    clean.data ,  
    group = participant,
    prop = 0.8, 
    strata = mo_ca
)
```

## Recipe

```{r}
reduced.recipe <- 
    recipes::recipe(performance_log ~ ., 
                    data = training(reduced.split)) %>% 
  update_role(participant, new_role = "id")%>%
  update_role(mo_ca, new_role = "external")%>%
    # convert categorical variables to factors
    recipes::step_string2factor(all_nominal()) %>%
    # combine low frequency factor levels
    recipes::step_other(all_nominal(), threshold = 0.001) %>%
    # remove no variance predictors which provide no predictive information 
    recipes::step_nzv(all_nominal()) %>%
    # prep the recipe so it can be used on other data
    prep()

model.formula <- formula(reduced.recipe)

filtered.recipe <- 
    recipes::recipe(mo_ca ~ ., 
                    data = training(filtered.split)) %>% 
  update_role(participant, new_role = "id")%>%
    # convert categorical variables to factors
    recipes::step_string2factor(all_nominal()) %>%
    # combine low frequency factor levels
    recipes::step_other(all_nominal(), threshold = 0.001) %>%
    # remove no variance predictors which provide no predictive information 
    recipes::step_nzv(all_nominal()) %>%
    # prep the recipe so it can be used on other data
    prep()

model.filtered.formula <- formula(filtered.recipe)

clean.recipe <-
    recipes::recipe(mo_ca ~ ., data = training(clean.split)) %>%
    # convert categorical variables to factors
    recipes::step_string2factor(all_nominal()) %>%
    # combine low frequency factor levels
    recipes::step_other(all_nominal(), threshold = 0.01) %>%
    # remove no variance predictors which provide no predictive information 
    recipes::step_nzv(all_nominal()) %>%
    # prep the recipe so it can be used on other data
    prep()
```

```{r}

control <- rfeControl(functions = rfFuncs, method = "cv", number = 5)

results <- rfe(
  x = train_processed[, -1],
  y = train_processed$target,
  sizes = c(10, 20, 30),
  rfeControl = control
)

selected_features <- predictors(results)
```


### Cross-validation folds

```{r}
reduced.cv.folds <- 
    recipes::bake(
        reduced.recipe, 
        new_data = training(reduced.split)
    ) %>%  
    rsample::group_vfold_cv(group=participant,v = 5)


filtered.cv.folds <- 
    recipes::bake(
        filtered.recipe, 
        new_data = training(filtered.split)
    ) %>%  
    rsample::group_vfold_cv(group=participant,v = 5)


clean.cv.folds <- 
    recipes::bake(
        clean.recipe, 
        new_data = training(clean.split)
    ) %>%  
    rsample::group_vfold_cv(group=participant,v = 5)
```

## Models

### Catboost

```{r}
catboost_model<- 
    parsnip::boost_tree(
        mode = "regression",
        trees = tune(),
        min_n = 5,
        learn_rate = tune(),
        tree_depth = tune()
    ) %>%
    set_engine("catboost", loss_function = "RMSE")

catboost_params <- 
    dials::parameters(
        trees(range = c(50,500)),
        tree_depth(range = c(4,10)), # depth
        # In most cases, the optimal depth ranges from 4 to 10. 
        # Values in the range from 6 to 10 are recommended. 
        learn_rate() # learning rate
    )

catboost.grid <- 
    dials::grid_space_filling(
        catboost_params, 
        size = 20 # set this to a higher number to get better results
        # I don't want to run this all night, so I set it to 30
    )

catboost.workflow <- 
    workflows::workflow() %>%
    add_model(catboost_model
             ) %>% 
    add_formula(mo_ca ~ .)
```

### XGBoost

```{r}
xgboost_model <- 
  parsnip::boost_tree(
    mode = "regression",
    trees = 1500,
    min_n = tune(),
    tree_depth = tune(),
    learn_rate = tune(),
    loss_reduction = tune()
  ) %>%
    set_engine("xgboost", objective = "reg:squarederror")

xgboost_params <- 
  dials::parameters(
    min_n(),
    tree_depth(),
    learn_rate(),
    loss_reduction()
  )

xgboost_grid <- 
  dials::grid_space_filling(
    xgboost_params, 
    size = 60
  )

xgboost_wf <- 
  workflows::workflow() %>%
  add_model(xgboost_model) %>%
  add_formula(model.formula)
```

### LightGBM

```{r}
lightgbm_model<- 
    parsnip::boost_tree(
        mode = "regression",
        trees = 1000,
        min_n = tune(),
        tree_depth = tune(),
    ) %>%
    set_engine("lightgbm", objective = "regression",verbose=-1)

lightgbm_params <- 
    dials::parameters(
      # The parameters have sane defaults, but if you have some knowledge 
      # of the process you can set upper and lower limits to these parameters.
        min_n(), # 2nd important
        tree_depth() # 3rd most important
    )

lgbm_grid <- 
    dials::grid_space_filling(
        lightgbm_params, 
        size = 30 # set this to a higher number to get better results
        # I don't want to run this all night, so I set it to 30
    )

lgbm_wf <- 
    workflows::workflow() %>%
    add_model(lightgbm_model
             ) %>% 
    add_formula(mo_ca ~ .)
```

# Training

## Catboost

```{r}
filtered.catboost.tuned <- tune::tune_grid(
    object = catboost.workflow,
    resamples = filtered.cv.folds,
    grid = catboost.grid,
    metrics = yardstick::metric_set(rmse, rsq, mae),
    control = tune::control_grid(verbose = T) # set this to TRUE to see
    # in what step of the process you are. But that doesn't look that well in
    # a blog.
)

filtered.catboost.tuned %>%
    tune::show_best(metric = "rmse",n = 5) 

filtered.catboost.tuned %>%  
  tune::show_best(metric = "rmse",n = 10) %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_longer(trees:learn_rate, names_to="variable",values_to="value" ) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(value,mean)) + 
  geom_line(alpha=1/2)+ 
  geom_point()+ 
  facet_wrap(~variable,scales = "free")+
  ggtitle("Best parameters for RMSE")

filtered.catboost.tuned %>%  
  tune::show_best(metric = "mae",n = 10) %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_longer(trees:learn_rate, names_to="variable",values_to="value" ) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(value,mean)) + 
  geom_line(alpha=1/2)+ 
  geom_point()+ 
  facet_wrap(~variable,scales = "free")+
  ggtitle("Best parameters for MAE")
```

```{r}
filtered.cbst.params <- 
  filtered.catboost.tuned %>%
    tune::select_best(metric="rmse")

filtered.cbst.model <- 
  catboost_model%>% 
  finalize_model(filtered.cbst.params)
```

```{r eval=F}
# create train set
filtered.train.processed <- bake(filtered.recipe,  new_data = training(filtered.split))
# fit model on entire trainset
trained.model.all.data <- filtered.cbst.model %>%
    # fit the model on all the training data
    fit(
        formula = mo_ca ~ ., 
        data    = filtered.train.processed
    )
train_prediction <- 
    trained.model.all.data %>% 
    predict(new_data = filtered.train.processed) %>%
    bind_cols(training(filtered.split))

train_prediction %>%
    yardstick::metrics(mo_ca, .pred) %>% 
  mutate(.estimate = format(round(.estimate, 2), big.mark = ",")) %>% 
  knitr::kable()
```

## XGBoost

```{r}
xgboost_tuned <- tune::tune_grid(
  object = xgboost_wf,
  resamples = reduced.cv.folds,
  grid = xgboost_grid,
  metrics = yardstick::metric_set(rmse, rsq, mae),
  control = tune::control_grid(verbose = TRUE)
)
xgboost_tuned %>%
  tune::show_best(metric = "rmse") %>%
  knitr::kable()

xgboost_best_params <- xgboost_tuned %>%
  tune::select_best(metric="rmse")

knitr::kable(xgboost_best_params)

xgboost_model_final <- xgboost_model %>% 
  finalize_model(xgboost_best_params)

train_processed <- bake(reduced.recipe,  new_data = training(reduced.split))

train_prediction <- xgboost_model_final %>%
  # fit the model on all the training data
  fit(
    formula = model.formula, 
    data    = train_processed
  ) %>%
  # predict the sale prices for the training data
  predict(new_data = train_processed) %>%
  bind_cols(training(reduced.split))

xgboost_score_train <- 
  train_prediction %>%
  yardstick::metrics(mo_ca, .pred) %>%
  mutate(.estimate = format(round(.estimate, 2), big.mark = ","))

knitr::kable(xgboost_score_train)
```
Lets Plot the feature importances now.

```{r}
# Fit final model (store it explicitly)
# Extract underlying xgboost model

final_fit <- xgboost_model_final %>%
  fit(formula = model.formula, data = train_processed)

# 2. Extract the underlying engine and plot importance
final_fit %>%
  extract_fit_engine() %>%
  vip(
    num_features = 10,          # Adjust based on your preference
    geom = "col",             # Can also use "col" for bars
    aesthetics = list(color = "steelblue", size = 3)
  ) +
  labs(title = "XGBoost Feature Importance (Tuned Model)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## LightGBM

```{r}
lgbm_tuned <- tune::tune_grid(
    object = lgbm_wf,
    resamples = filtered.cv.folds,
    grid = lgbm_grid,
    metrics = yardstick::metric_set(rmse, rsq, mae),
    control = tune::control_grid(verbose = T) # set this to TRUE to see
    # in what step of the process you are. But that doesn't look that well in
    # a blog.
)

lgbm_tuned %>%
    tune::show_best(metric = "rmse",n = 5) 

lgbm_tuned %>%  
  tune::show_best(metric = "rmse",n = 10) %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_longer(min_n:tree_depth, names_to="variable",values_to="value" ) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(value,mean)) + 
  geom_line(alpha=1/2)+ 
  geom_point()+ 
  facet_wrap(~variable,scales = "free")+
  ggtitle("Best parameters for RMSE")

lgbm_tuned %>%  
  tune::show_best(metric = "mae",n = 10) %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_longer(min_n:tree_depth, names_to="variable",values_to="value" ) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(value,mean)) + 
  geom_line(alpha=1/2)+ 
  geom_point()+ 
  facet_wrap(~variable,scales = "free")+
  ggtitle("Best parameters for MAE")

lgbm_best_params <- 
  lgbm_tuned %>%
    tune::select_best(metric = "rmse")

lgbm_model_final <- 
  lightgbm_model%>% 
  finalize_model(lgbm_best_params)
```

```{r}
# Fit final model
lgbm_fit <- lgbm_model_final %>%
  fit(
    formula = model.formula,
    data    = train_processed
  )

# Extract engine
lgbm_engine <- lgbm_fit$fit

# Feature importance
library(lightgbm)

importance_matrix <- lightgbm::lgb.importance(lgbm_engine)

# Table
importance_tbl <- importance_matrix %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(Gain))

knitr::kable(importance_tbl)

# Plot
lightgbm::lgb.plot.importance(importance_matrix)
```

# Test

## Catboost

```{r}
test_processed  <- bake(filtered.recipe, new_data = testing(filtered.split))
test_prediction <- 
    trained.model.all.data %>% 
    # use the training model fit to predict the test data
    predict(new_data = test_processed) %>%
    bind_cols(testing(filtered.split))

test_prediction %>%
    yardstick::metrics(mo_ca, .pred) %>% 
  mutate(.estimate = format(round(.estimate, 2), big.mark = ",")) %>% 
  knitr::kable()

house_prediction_residual <- test_prediction %>%
    arrange(.pred) %>%
    mutate(residual_moca = (mo_ca - .pred) / .pred) %>%
    select(.pred, residual_moca)

ggplot(house_prediction_residual, aes(x = .pred, y = residual_moca)) +
    geom_point() +
    xlab("MoCa Score") +
    ylab("Residual (%)") +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::number) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)

```

## XGBoost

```{r}

test_processed  <- bake(reduced.recipe, new_data = testing(reduced.split))
test_prediction <- xgboost_model_final %>%
  # fit the model on all the training data
  fit(
    formula = model.formula, 
    data    = train_processed
  ) %>%
  # use the training model fit to predict the test data
  predict(new_data = test_processed) %>%
  bind_cols(testing(reduced.split))

# measure the accuracy of our model using `yardstick`
xgboost_score <- 
  test_prediction %>%
  yardstick::metrics(mo_ca, .pred) %>%
  mutate(.estimate = format(round(.estimate, 2), big.mark = ","))

knitr::kable(xgboost_score)

house_prediction_residual <- test_prediction %>%
  arrange(.pred) %>%
  mutate(residual_moca = (mo_ca - .pred) / .pred) %>%
  select(.pred, residual_moca)

ggplot(house_prediction_residual, aes(x = .pred, y = residual_moca)) +
  geom_point() +
  xlab("MoCa Score") +
  ylab("Residual (%)") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::number) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)
```

## LightGBM

```{r}
test_processed  <- bake(filtered.recipe, new_data = testing(filtered.split))

test_prediction <- lgbm_model_final %>%
  # fit the model on all the training data
  fit(
    formula = mo_ca ~ ., 
    data    = train_processed
  ) %>%
  # use the training model fit to predict the test data
  predict(new_data = test_processed) %>%
  bind_cols(testing(filtered.split))

# measure the accuracy of our model using `yardstick`
lightgbm_score <- 
  test_prediction %>%
  yardstick::metrics(mo_ca, .pred) %>%
  mutate(.estimate = format(round(.estimate, 2), big.mark = ","))

knitr::kable(lightgbm_score)

house_prediction_residual <- test_prediction %>%
  arrange(.pred) %>%
  mutate(residual_moca = (mo_ca - .pred) / .pred) %>%
  select(.pred, residual_moca)

ggplot(house_prediction_residual, aes(x = .pred, y = residual_moca)) +
  geom_point() +
  xlab("MoCa Score") +
  ylab("Residual (%)") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::number) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)
```

# SHAP Shapley Additive Explanations

```{r}
df_test <- testing(reduced.split)
xvars <- names(df_test)[-1]
X_explain <- df_test[, xvars]  # Use only feature columns


fit <- xgboost_model_final %>%
    # fit the model on all the training data
    fit(
        formula = model.formula, 
        data    = train_processed
    )


# 1.5 minutes on laptop
# Note: If you have more than p=8 features, use kernelshap() instead of permshap()
system.time(
  shap_values <- fit |> 
    permshap(X = X_explain, 
             feature_names = xvars,
             exact = FALSE) |> 
    shapviz()
)
 saveRDS(shap_values, file = "shap_values.rds")
 shap_values <- readRDS("shap_values.rds")

shap_values |> 
  sv_importance("bee", max_display = 15,size = 0.5)

shap_values |> 
  sv_dependence(c("interviewer","l_mean","ba","physical_activity","sleep","aid_strength_left"), color_var = "auto")


shap_values |> 
  sv_force(row_id = 5)


shap_values |> 
  sv_waterfall(row_id = c(40))


sv_importance(shap_values, kind = "both", show_numbers = TRUE)
sv_dependence(shap, "carat", color_var = "auto")
sv_dependence(shap, "clarity", color_var = "auto")
sv_force(shap, row_id = 1)
sv_waterfall(shap, row_id = 1)
```
